name: A.G. BUYERS
description_short: Family-owned antique and estate buyer with three NY locations offering same-day cash for jewelry, coins, military items, and collectibles since the 2000s.
description_long:
Antique & Estate Buyers is a family-owned and operated company that has served the New York Tri-State Area (New York, New Jersey, and Connecticut) since the 2000s. The company specializes in purchasing antiques, collectibles, estate jewelry, and military artifacts from individual customers and estates. With three physical locations in Manhattan, Lindenhurst, and Smithtown, they position themselves as a convenient alternative to traditional liquidation methods for customers downsizing, moving, or liquidating collections.

The company offers free appraisals and same-day cash payments for a wide range of items including antique jewelry, coins, military artifacts, watches, fine art, sterling silver flatware, furniture, lamps, and collectibles. They claim to have served over 3,000 customers and emphasize their expertise in accurately evaluating item worth. House calls and same-day service are available for customers unable to visit locations. Their business model focuses on converting "old stuff" into immediate cash for customers with space or liquidity needs.

A.G. Buyers differentiates itself through stated expertise in military antiques and coins, emphasizing historical preservation and market knowledge. They highlight decades of experience as antique dealers and promise "top dollars" and competitive pricing. The company operates extended hours (8 AM - 10 PM by appointment only) and maintains multiple convenient locations. Their marketing emphasizes customer reviews and family business credibility.

While the website projects professionalism and customer focus, several limitations should be noted. As a buyer rather than lender, they don't offer the credit or financial assistance products implied by consumer finance categorization. Pricing transparency is absent—no examples of valuations or purchase offers are provided. The reliance on appointments and subjective appraisals means actual offers may differ significantly from expectations. Customers should verify credentials and get independent appraisals before selling items of significant value.
pros: Three convenient physical locations (Manhattan, Lindenhurst, Smithtown) across the NY Tri-State Area
Free appraisals and same-day cash payments on the spot
House calls and estate purchase service available—no need to transport large collections
Extended business hours (8 AM - 10 PM) operating by appointment
Specialization in military antiques with stated expertise in historical significance and market value
Decades of operational history (since 2000s) with claim of 3,000+ customer relationships
Accepts single items or entire estate collections without minimum purchase requirements
cons: No pricing transparency—website provides no examples of valuations or typical cash offers, making it impossible to assess competitiveness
Appraisals are subjective and conducted by their own staff with financial incentive to undervalue items
Appointment-only model creates friction and barriers to quick transactions despite same-day cash claims
No verifiable credentials, certifications, or third-party ratings displayed on website despite claims of 'highly rated reviews'
As a buyer rather than lender, they don't provide actual financial products—only asset liquidation
best_for: Estate executors and heirs liquidating deceased relatives' antiques, jewelry, and collectibles quickly
Homeowners downsizing or moving who need rapid cash for unwanted items without shipping hassle
Military history enthusiasts and collectors wanting to sell specialized artifacts to knowledgeable buyers
Individuals with antique coins or jewelry seeking local evaluation and immediate cash without consignment delays
diagnosis: A.G. Buyers is best suited for people with antiques, jewelry, or estate items who need quick liquidation and prefer local, in-person transactions over online marketplaces or consignment. The main caveat is that transparent pricing and independent verification are essential before accepting any offer, as their valuations are internal and not subject to market competition or third-party verification.
services: Free appraisals for antiques, jewelry, and collectibles
Same-day cash purchases and payments on the spot
House calls for estate purchases and in-home evaluations
Antique military artifact buying and valuation
Fine art and painting evaluation and purchase
Collectibles buying (watches, sterling silver flatware, furniture, lamps)
Entire estate liquidation and purchase services
Tri-State Area service coverage (NY, NJ, CT)
